title: Mild Italian Bread Tagine
description: A tangy and light Italian dish from Puglia featuring stale bread, char-grilled flavors, and anti-inflammatory friendly ingredients, cooked in a tagine pot.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Cut the stale bread into 1-inch cubes and place on a baking sheet.
Drizzle the olive oil over the bread and toss to coat.
Char-grill the bread in the oven for 10 minutes, or until lightly toasted.
In a large tagine pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the thinly sliced red onion and cook for 3 minutes, until translucent.
Add the canned tomatoes, chopped fresh basil, red pepper flakes, salt, black pepper, and turmeric to the pot.
Stir to combine and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Add the char-grilled bread to the pot and stir to combine.
Cover the pot and transfer to the preheated oven.
